name: Grilled Dried Beef (Thit Bo Kho)
recipeIngredient: 1 pound Lean bottom round or sirloin
2 stalks fresh lemon grass or 2 tablespoons dried lemon grass
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the beef across the grain into very thin 3 by 3 inch slices.
2. If using fresh lemon grass, discard the outer leaves and upper half of the stalk; cut into thin slices and finely chop. If using dried lemon grass, soak in warm water for 1 hour, drain, and finely chop.
3. Combine the chiles and sugar in a mortar and pestle and pound to a fine paste. Add the chopped lemon grass, fish sauce, and soy sauce and stir to blend. (Alternatively, combine all ingredients in a blender and blend to a very fine paste.)
4. Spread the paste over the beef pieces to coat both sides. Let marinate for 30 minutes.
5. Spread each slice of marinated beef on a large, flat wire rack or baking sheet. Let stand in the sun until both sides are completely dried, about 12 hours. Alternatively, place a rack on a jelly roll pan and let the beef dry in the refrigerator for 2 days.
6. Grill the beef over a medium charcoal fire or transfer the rack to the middle of a preheated 400°F oven and bake until brown and crisp, about 10 minutes.
7. Serve with glutinous rice.
NOTE: After cooking, the meat may be kept for up to 1 week in a covered jar at room temperature.
